headlines.title: Bank of Japan rate increase a “done deal,” course ahead uncertain
Japan's Nikkei recoups early losses as US signals end of Iran strikes
Japanese Yen moves little after reaching six-week lows
The Bank of Japan is set to hold its first-ever meeting without a governor, causing some market unease—not over fears of a rate hike, but over concerns of not understanding the messaging.
Japan's Nikkei recoups early losses as US signals end of Iran strikes
U.S.-Iran tensions flare up again, Asian stocks decline; Japanese equities fall 1.5%, while South Korean shares plunge as much as 4%.

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 미-이란 긴장 고조로 하락 출발했으나, 긴장 완화 신호에 힘입어 낙폭을 대부분 만회하며 변동성 장세를 보였습니다. 일본은행(BOJ)의 금리 인상은 기정사실로 여겨지지만, 향후 정책 방향에 대한 불확실성이 상존합니다. 특히 총재 부재 상태로 열릴 BOJ 회의에 대한 시장의 불안감은 금리 인상 자체보다는 메시지 전달의 불확실성에 대한 우려로 나타났습니다. 엔화는 6주래 최저치 기록 후 큰 움직임을 보이지 않았습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese stocks experienced a volatile day, initially falling due to renewed US-Iran tensions but largely recouping losses after signals of de-escalation. The Bank of Japan's impending rate hike is widely considered a 'done deal,' yet uncertainty persists regarding its future policy course. Market unease is also noted over an upcoming BOJ meeting without a governor, raising concerns about clear communication rather than the rate hike itself. The Yen showed little movement after reaching six-week lows.
